heading: Sharing of cyber threat indicators and defensive measures with the Federal Government
label: 1504 Sharing of cyber threat indicators and defensive measures with the Federal Government

children.heading: Requirement for policies and procedures
children.label: a Requirement for policies and procedures

children.heading: Interim policies and procedures
children.label: 1 Interim policies and procedures
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall, in consultation with the heads of the appropriate Federal entities, jointly develop and submit to Congress interim policies and procedures relating to the receipt of cyber threat indicators and defensive measures by the Federal Government.December 18, 2015

children.heading: Final policies and procedures
children.label: 2 Final policies and procedures
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall, in consultation with the heads of the appropriate Federal entities, jointly issue and make publicly available final policies and procedures relating to the receipt of cyber threat indicators and defensive measures by the Federal Government.December 18, 2015

children.label: II

children.label: III

children.label: ii

children.heading: Guidelines for entities sharing cyber threat indicators with Federal Government
children.label: 4 Guidelines for entities sharing cyber threat indicators with Federal Government

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall jointly develop and make publicly available guidance to assist entities and promote sharing of cyber threat indicators with Federal entities under this subchapter.December 18, 2015

children.label: I
children.text: is not directly related to a cybersecurity threat; and

children.label: II
children.text: is personal information of a specific individual or information that identifies a specific individual.

children.label: ii
children.text: Identification of types of information protected under otherwise applicable privacy laws that are unlikely to be directly related to a cybersecurity threat.

children.label: iii
children.text: Such other matters as the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security consider appropriate for entities sharing cyber threat indicators with Federal entities under this subchapter.

children.heading: Interim guidelines
children.label: 1 Interim guidelines
children.text: Not later than 60 days after , the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall, in consultation with heads of the appropriate Federal entities and in consultation with officers designated under , jointly develop, submit to Congress, and make available to the public interim guidelines relating to privacy and civil liberties which shall govern the receipt, retention, use, and dissemination of cyber threat indicators by a Federal entity obtained in connection with activities authorized in this subchapter.December 18, 2015section 2000ee–1 of title 42

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall, in coordination with heads of the appropriate Federal entities and in consultation with officers designated under and such private entities with industry expertise as the Attorney General and the Secretary consider relevant, jointly issue and make publicly available final guidelines relating to privacy and civil liberties which shall govern the receipt, retention, use, and dissemination of cyber threat indicators by a Federal entity obtained in connection with activities authorized in this subchapter.December 18, 2015section 2000ee–1 of title 42

children.heading: Periodic review
children.label: B Periodic review
children.text: The Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security shall, in coordination with heads of the appropriate Federal entities and in consultation with officers and private entities described in subparagraph (A), periodically, but not less frequently than once every 2 years, jointly review the guidelines issued under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: limit the effect on privacy and civil liberties of activities by the Federal Government under this subchapter;

children.label: i
children.text: a process for the timely destruction of such information that is known not to be directly related to uses authorized under this subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: specific limitations on the length of any period in which a cyber threat indicator may be retained;

children.label: C
children.text: include requirements to safeguard cyber threat indicators containing personal information of specific individuals or information that identifies specific individuals from unauthorized access or acquisition, including appropriate sanctions for activities by officers, employees, or agents of the Federal Government in contravention of such guidelines;

children.label: D
children.text: consistent with this subchapter, any other applicable provisions of law, and the fair information practice principles set forth in appendix A of the document entitled “National Strategy for Trusted Identities in Cyberspace” and published by the President in April 2011, govern the retention, use, and dissemination by the Federal Government of cyber threat indicators shared with the Federal Government under this subchapter, including the extent, if any, to which such cyber threat indicators may be used by the Federal Government;

children.label: E
children.text: include procedures for notifying entities and Federal entities if information received pursuant to this section is known or determined by a Federal entity receiving such information not to constitute a cyber threat indicator;

children.label: F
children.text: protect the confidentiality of cyber threat indicators containing personal information of specific individuals or information that identifies specific individuals to the greatest extent practicable and require recipients to be informed that such indicators may only be used for purposes authorized under this subchapter; and

children.label: G
children.text: include steps that may be needed so that dissemination of cyber threat indicators is consistent with the protection of classified and other sensitive national security information.

children.heading: Capability and process within the Department of Homeland Security
children.label: c Capability and process within the Department of Homeland Security
A
children.text: shall accept from any non-Federal entity in real time cyber threat indicators and defensive measures, pursuant to this section;

children.label: i
children.text: consistent with , communications between a Federal entity and a non-Federal entity regarding a previously shared cyber threat indicator to describe the relevant cybersecurity threat or develop a defensive measure based on such cyber threat indicator; andsection 1503 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: communications by a regulated non-Federal entity with such entity’s Federal regulatory authority regarding a cybersecurity threat;

children.label: C
children.text: ensures that all of the appropriate Federal entities receive in an automated manner such cyber threat indicators and defensive measures shared through the real-time process within the Department of Homeland Security;

children.label: D
children.text: is in compliance with the policies, procedures, and guidelines required by this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: reporting of known or suspected criminal activity, by a non-Federal entity to any other non-Federal entity or a Federal entity, including cyber threat indicators or defensive measures shared with a Federal entity in furtherance of opening a Federal law enforcement investigation;

children.label: ii
children.text: voluntary or legally compelled participation in a Federal investigation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: providing cyber threat indicators or defensive measures as part of a statutory or authorized contractual requirement.

children.heading: Certification of capability and process
children.label: A Certification of capability and process
i
children.text: as the process by which the Federal Government receives from any non-Federal entity a cyber threat indicator or defensive measure under this subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in accordance with the interim policies, procedures, and guidelines developed under this subchapter.

children.label: I
children.text: such designation is necessary to ensure that full, effective, and secure operation of a capability and process for the Federal Government to receive from any non-Federal entity cyber threat indicators or defensive measures under this subchapter;

children.label: II
children.text: the designated appropriate Federal entity will receive and share cyber threat indicators and defensive measures in accordance with the policies, procedures, and guidelines developed under this subchapter, including subsection (a)(3)(A); and

children.label: III
children.text: such designation is consistent with the mission of such appropriate Federal entity and improves the ability of the Federal Government to receive, share, and use cyber threat indicators and defensive measures as authorized under this subchapter.

children.heading: Application to additional capability and process
children.label: ii Application to additional capability and process
children.text: If the President designates an appropriate Federal entity to develop and implement a capability and process under clause (i), the provisions of this subchapter that apply to the capability and process required by paragraph (1) shall also be construed to apply to the capability and process developed and implemented under clause (i).

children.label: A
children.text: any non-Federal entity may share cyber threat indicators and defensive measures through such process with the Federal Government; and

children.label: B
children.text: all of the appropriate Federal entities receive such cyber threat indicators and defensive measures in real time with receipt through the process within the Department of Homeland Security consistent with the policies and procedures issued under subsection (a).

children.heading: Other Federal entities
children.label: 4 Other Federal entities
children.text: The process developed and implemented under paragraph (1) shall ensure that other Federal entities receive in a timely manner any cyber threat indicators and defensive measures shared with the Federal Government through such process.

children.heading: Information shared with or provided to the Federal Government
children.label: d Information shared with or provided to the Federal Government

children.heading: No waiver of privilege or protection
children.label: 1 No waiver of privilege or protection
children.text: The provision of cyber threat indicators and defensive measures to the Federal Government under this subchapter shall not constitute a waiver of any applicable privilege or protection provided by law, including trade secret protection.

children.heading: Proprietary information
children.label: 2 Proprietary information
children.text: Consistent with and any other applicable provision of law, a cyber threat indicator or defensive measure provided by a non-Federal entity to the Federal Government under this subchapter shall be considered the commercial, financial, and proprietary information of such non-Federal entity when so designated by the originating non-Federal entity or a third party acting in accordance with the written authorization of the originating non-Federal entity.section 1503(c)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: deemed voluntarily shared information and exempt from disclosure under and any State, tribal, or local provision of law requiring disclosure of information or records; andsection 552 of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: withheld, without discretion, from the public under and any State, tribal, or local provision of law requiring disclosure of information or records.section 552(b)(3)(B) of title 5

children.heading: Ex parte communications
children.label: 4 Ex parte communications
children.text: The provision of a cyber threat indicator or defensive measure to the Federal Government under this subchapter shall not be subject to a rule of any Federal agency or department or any judicial doctrine regarding ex parte communications with a decision-making official.

children.label: i
children.text: a cybersecurity purpose;

children.label: I
children.text: a cybersecurity threat, including the source of such cybersecurity threat; or

children.label: II
children.text: a security vulnerability;

children.label: iii
children.text: the purpose of responding to, or otherwise preventing or mitigating, a specific threat of death, a specific threat of serious bodily harm, or a specific threat of serious economic harm, including a terrorist act or a use of a weapon of mass destruction;

children.label: iv
children.text: the purpose of responding to, investigating, prosecuting, or otherwise preventing or mitigating, a serious threat to a minor, including sexual exploitation and threats to physical safety; or

children.label: I
children.text: sections 1028 through 1030 of title 18 (relating to fraud and identity theft);

children.label: II
children.text: chapter 37 of such title (relating to espionage and censorship); and

children.label: III
children.text: chapter 90 of such title (relating to protection of trade secrets).

children.heading: Prohibited activities
children.label: B Prohibited activities
children.text: Cyber threat indicators and defensive measures provided to the Federal Government under this subchapter shall not be disclosed to, retained by, or used by any Federal agency or department for any use not permitted under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: in accordance with the policies, procedures, and guidelines required by subsections (a) and (b);

children.label: I
children.text: personal information of a specific individual; or

children.label: II
children.text: information that identifies a specific individual; and

children.label: I
children.text: personal information of a specific individual; or

children.label: II
children.text: information that identifies a specific individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), cyber threat indicators and defensive measures provided to the Federal Government under this subchapter shall not be used by any Federal, State, tribal, or local government to regulate, including an enforcement action, the lawful activities of any non-Federal entity or any activities taken by a non-Federal entity pursuant to mandatory standards, including activities relating to monitoring, operating defensive measures, or sharing cyber threat indicators.

children.heading: Regulatory authority specifically relating to prevention or mitigation of cybersecurity threats
children.label: I Regulatory authority specifically relating to prevention or mitigation of cybersecurity threats
children.text: Cyber threat indicators and defensive measures provided to the Federal Government under this subchapter may, consistent with Federal or State regulatory authority specifically relating to the prevention or mitigation of cybersecurity threats to information systems, inform the development or implementation of regulations relating to such information systems.

children.heading: Procedures developed and implemented under this subchapter
children.label: II Procedures developed and implemented under this subchapter
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to procedures developed and implemented under this subchapter.
